meta.title: Early Seizure Prediction After CVT
meta.cff_info:
Early Seizure Risk Nomogram in Cerebral Venous Thrombosis (CVT)

Fang et al., 2021

DOI: 10.1016/j.eplepsyres.2021.106820

Factors and Points

  • GCS ≤ 8:
    • Defined as a Glasgow Coma Scale score of 8 or lower at admission.
    • Odds Ratio: 4.784 (95% CI: 1.640–13.970)
    • Points: 100
  • Frontal Lobe Lesion:
    • Frontal lobe involvement on CT or MRI at diagnosis.
    • Odds Ratio: 3.179 (95% CI: 1.160–8.714)
    • Points: 74
  • Superior Sagittal Sinus Thrombosis (SSST):
    • Thrombus in the superior sagittal sinus confirmed by imaging.
    • Odds Ratio: 2.886 (95% CI: 1.090–7.642)
    • Points: 68
  • Hemorrhagic Lesion:
    • Presence of intracerebral hemorrhage on baseline neuroimaging.
    • Odds Ratio: 2.555 (95% CI: 1.032–6.330)
    • Points: 60
  • Focal Neurological Deficit:
    • Any focal neurologic sign noted at admission (e.g., hemiparesis, aphasia).
    • Odds Ratio: 2.240 (95% CI: 0.899–5.586)
    • Points: 52

Risk Estimates

Estimated early seizure probability based on total nomogram score:

Total Points Predicted Risk of Early Seizure
< 37< 10%
37 – 8810–20%
89 – 12220–30%
123 – 15030–40%
151 – 17640–50%
177 – 20250–60%
203 – 23060–70%
231 – 26470–80%
265 – 31780–90%
≥ 318> 90%

Clinical Utility

This nomogram enables clinicians to estimate the risk of acute symptomatic seizures in patients with cerebral venous thrombosis based on five readily available clinical and imaging variables.

Additional Study Information

Category Details
Score Name CVT Early Seizure Risk Nomogram
Population 142 patients with confirmed CVT (2013–2018, Tongji Hospital)
Statistical Methods Multivariate logistic regression
Validation Methods Internal bootstrap validation (200 replicates), GiViTI calibration belt, decision curve analysis
Performance Metrics C-index: 0.835
Clinical Utility Supports early risk stratification and targeted seizure prevention in CVT patients
meta.authors: Fang Y, Song G, Lin J, et al.
meta.outcome: Predicting the occurrence of early seizures after cerebral venous thrombosis using a comprehensive nomogram

predictors.name: GCS ≤ 8 at admission
predictors.assess: GCS ≤ 8 at admission is included in the model with an odds ratio of 4.784 (95% confidence interval: 1.640–13.970) and contributes approximately 100 points to the nomogram.

predictors.name: Frontal lobe lesion
predictors.assess: Defined as radiologically confirmed involvement of the frontal lobe on initial CT or MRI, as assessed by the attending neuroradiologist. Lesion types included infarction, edema, or hemorrhage affecting the frontal cortex. OR: 3.179 (95% CI: 1.160–8.714) Nomogram contribution: ~74 points

predictors.name: Superior sagittal sinus thrombosis (SSST)
predictors.assess: Defined as presence of thrombus in the superior sagittal sinus, confirmed by imaging (CT venography, MR venography, or DSA). SSST status was determined from radiology reports at diagnosis. OR: 2.886 (95% CI: 1.090–7.642) Nomogram contribution: ~68 points

predictors.name: Hemorrhagic lesion
predictors.assess: Defined as any hemorrhagic transformation or primary intracerebral hemorrhage visible on baseline imaging (CT or MRI) upon diagnosis of cerebral venous thrombosis (CVT). OR: 2.555 (95% CI: 1.032–6.330) Nomogram contribution: ~60 points

predictors.name: Focal neurological deficit
predictors.assess: Defined as the presence of any localized neurological impairment documented on clinical examination at admission, including but not limited to hemiparesis, aphasia, or visual field deficits. OR: 2.240 (95% CI: 0.899–5.586) Nomogram contribution: ~52 points

provenance.notes: Predictors/points/rows from CFF export; complete meta from paper; verify vs live form.
